concerns.text: FR-001 mandates identifying '20-30 morally ambiguous scenarios'. This specific numeric range contradicts the 'Measurable Outcomes' preamble which states: 'Planning docs state *what* will be measured... defer specific empirical values (counts, dataset sizes...) to the implementation/research phase.' The spec should either remove the '20-30' constraint or mark it as '[deferred]' to maintain internal consistency with the project's planning conventions.

SC-003 references a sample size of 'N=100-150'. Similar to FR-001, this specific empirical value (count) contradicts the 'Measurable Outcomes' preamble instruction to defer specific counts to the implementation phase. The requirement should be rephrased to measure power against 'the planned sample size' or use the '[deferred]' marker.

The Assumptions section lists 'The sample size of 100-150 participants is sufficient...'. While assumptions can contain estimates, the presence of specific numbers here alongside FR-001 and SC-003 creates a scope shift where the spec treats these as fixed requirements rather than deferred variables, conflicting with the 'Measurable Outcomes' preamble.
